title: AI helped me find a critical gap in my literature review
post:
I spent three months on my literature review before bringing in AI. I thought I had a comprehensive picture of my field. I was wrong.

AI surfaced a paper from 2019 that had been cited minimally but directly addressed a limitation I was planning to spend two years working around. It wasn't in any of my keyword searches because the terminology had evolved. I would have wasted significant time and resources without this catch.

This is AI at its best: augmenting human expertise, not replacing it. I know the field. AI helped me see what I missed.

How do you currently handle literature reviews? Have you considered AI assistance? Thoughts?
